text:
CMS Common Medicare Environment (CME) 

VALUES: Y = Employer subsidized for the retired beneficiary 
N = No employer subsidization for the retired beneficiary 
0 = Not Medicare enrolled for the month 
Null/missing = Enrolled in Medicare A and/or B, but no Part D enrollment data for the beneficiary. 

COMMENT: Some employers offer prescription drug plans to their retirees, and Part D pays a subsidy to plans that 
offer coverage that is equivalent to (or better than) conventional Part D benefits.
